name: Luhansk People's Republic

colors.symbolism: the sky over the steppe; also the protection of the Theotokos in Slavic Orthodox tradition
the waters of the Donets and Luhanka rivers
the blood shed in the struggle for independence; the historic colour of Russian and Soviet revolutionary movements

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — light blue over blue over red — without charges (the plain civil/state form).
history: Adopted by the People's Council of the Luhansk People's Republic following the republic's self-proclamation on 27 April 2014. Earlier ceremonial versions carried a two-headed eagle and the inscription Луганская Народная Республика; the simplified tricolour is used as the civil flag and is the form most commonly seen in public display. The LPR was recognised internationally only by Russia (until 2022), South Ossetia, and a handful of other partly-recognised states, and was formally annexed by Russia as a federal subject on 30 September 2022. International consensus continues to treat the territory as occupied Ukrainian territory (Luhansk Oblast).
